page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-96 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.6 GHz, with hyperthreading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. The processor configuration includes 48 KB of L1d cache, 1 MB of L2 cache, and 54 MB of L3 cache. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. While single-core performance metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core, fall into the average range, multi-core execution is strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth is also strong, reaching 328.7 GB/sec for large-block read operations and 144.82 GB/sec for large-block write operations. However, workloads requiring high-capacity LLM inference show significant performance tradeoffs; text generation using a large 70B model is rated as poor, achieving only 2.34 tokens per second, whereas prompt processing on smaller models remains strong.

Given its balanced resource allocation and strong multi-threaded capabilities, this instance is suited for parallelized workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and static web serving. It also demonstrates strong performance in database operations and Redis SET operations. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-standard-96 offers high resource density for multi-threaded applications that can leverage all 96 vCPUs, though the lack of local storage requires external block storage provisioning, which should be factored into the overall architectural design.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-96 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.6 GHz. It provides 4.0 GB of memory per core and does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results show average single-core performance but strong multi-core execution, placing in the top 25% for multi-core stress-ng, Geekbench, and PassMark tests. Memory bandwidth is also strong, with uncached reads reaching 328.7 GB/sec. However, the instance exhibits poor performance for large-scale LLM text generation, making it less suitable for heavy AI inference. It is highly effective for parallelized workloads, including software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-96 is an x86_64 instance with 96 vCPUs and 384 GB RAM. It delivers strong multi-core performance and memory bandwidth, though single-core and LLM text generation are limited.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-96 is a general-purpose x86_64 instance with 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and strong multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs and strong multi-core performance for parallelized workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs and 384 GB of RAM on Intel Xeon architecture
Strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth
Average single-core execution and poor large-model LLM text generation
No local storage or integrated GPU accelerators included
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving
